title: Fixing CSS Pseudo-Element Styles with Inconsistent Box Model

statement.markdown: You are given a CSS string containing styles for pseudo-elements (e.g., ::before, ::after). The goal is to fix the bug in the CSS string, which causes inconsistent box model styles. The styles may include width, height, padding, margin, border, and box-sizing properties. Your task is to write a function that takes the CSS string as input and returns the corrected CSS string with consistent box model styles.
statement.inputFormat: A CSS string as a string, containing styles for pseudo-elements.
statement.outputFormat: The corrected CSS string as a string, with consistent box model styles.
statement.notes: The input CSS string may contain various styles, including width, height, padding, margin, border, and box-sizing properties. You should ensure that the box model styles are consistent across all pseudo-elements.

constraints.description: Maximum number of pseudo-elements in the input CSS string.

examples.input:
::before { width: 100px; height: 50px; padding: 10px; margin: 20px; border: 1px solid black; }
::after { width: 200px; height: 100px; padding: 20px; margin: 40px; border: 2px solid red; }
examples.output:
::before { box-sizing: border-box; width: 100px; height: 50px; padding: 10px; margin: 20px; border: 1px solid black; }
::after { box-sizing: border-box; width: 200px; height: 100px; padding: 20px; margin: 40px; border: 2px solid red; }
examples.explanation: The input CSS string contains inconsistent box model styles. The corrected output CSS string ensures consistent box model styles for both pseudo-elements.

hints.text: First hint text: Check the box-sizing property for consistency.

editorial.title: Editorial title
editorial.intuition: Explanation of the correct approach: Ensure consistent box model styles for all pseudo-elements by setting the box-sizing property to 'border-box'.
editorial.bruteForce: Brute force approach summary: Manually inspect each pseudo-element style and adjust the box-sizing property accordingly.
editorial.optimalSolution: Optimal solution details: Use a regular expression to find and replace the box-sizing property in the input CSS string.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: Correctness proof: Verify that the corrected CSS string produces the expected output for various input styles.
editorial.dryRun: Step-by-step example run: Walk through the solution with a sample input CSS string.

testCases.input:
::before { width: 100px; height: 50px; padding: 10px; margin: 20px; border: 1px solid black; }
::after { width: 200px; height: 100px; padding: 20px; margin: 40px; border: 2px solid red; }
testCases.output:
::before { box-sizing: border-box; width: 100px; height: 50px; padding: 10px; margin: 20px; border: 1px solid black; }
::after { box-sizing: border-box; width: 200px; height: 100px; padding: 20px; margin: 40px; border: 2px solid red; }

testCases.input:
::before { width: 50px; height: 25px; padding: 5px; margin: 10px; border: 1px solid green; }
::after { width: 150px; height: 75px; padding: 15px; margin: 30px; border: 3px solid blue; }
testCases.output:
::before { box-sizing: border-box; width: 50px; height: 25px; padding: 5px; margin: 10px; border: 1px solid green; }
::after { box-sizing: border-box; width: 150px; height: 75px; padding: 15px; margin: 30px; border: 3px solid blue; }
